checks.describe: the Deep Dive's KServe InferenceService read is a well-formed spec walk: the sample manifest carries kind InferenceService, a predictor, and the canaryTrafficPercent field that IS the abstraction the M9 router did by hand (Deep Dive: KServe anatomy). Read from the deep-dive page's embedded YAML captured to a file for the check
checks.run: F=labs/m9/kserve-sample.yaml; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'kind: InferenceService' "$F" && grep -q 'predictor' "$F" && grep -q 'canaryTrafficPercent' "$F"; then echo kserve-shape-ok; else echo kserve-shape-missing; fi

checks.describe: the split_clients weight math the Deep Dive works out is real: a 10% canary bucket over N requests converges toward 0.1*N, and the small-N illusion (0.9^20 ~ 12% chance of zero canary hits) is a computable fact, not a hand-wave (Deep Dive: reading a split). Verified by a tiny arithmetic check
checks.run: python3 -c "p=0.9**20; import sys; print('illusion-real' if 0.10 < p < 0.15 else 'illusion-wrong')"

checks.describe: the llm-d / Gateway API Inference Extension read is anchored to the real object shapes: the frontier sample carries an InferencePool and the KV/prefix-aware routing idea, and the disaggregated prefill/decode pools callback to M1's two phases (Deep Dive: the frontier). Read from the captured frontier sample
checks.run: F=labs/m9/inferencepool-sample.yaml; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'InferencePool' "$F" && grep -qi 'prefill' "$F" && grep -qi 'decode' "$F"; then echo frontier-shape-ok; else echo frontier-shape-missing; fi

checks.describe: the decision tree in the Deep Dive walks all four branches with the dependency-cost ladder and the two things the router pattern CANNOT do (per-token / cache-aware routing, prefix affinity) — the reason the frontier exists (Deep Dive: the tree, branch by branch)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/DECISION-TREE.md; if grep -qi 'per-token' "$F" && grep -qi 'prefix affinity' "$F" && grep -qi 'dependency' "$F" && grep -q 'InferencePool' "$F"; then echo branches-ok; else echo branches-missing; fi
